preview_text:
The model backs Croatia to win with a 78% probability, leaving Ghana with only a 3% chance of victory. This represents a substantial gap: the model views Croatia as a heavy favorite in this neutral-venue matchup.

The model's spread reflects a confident but not overwhelming outcome distribution. A draw sits at 19%, suggesting the match carries genuine competitive uncertainty in the opening stages, but Croatia's win probability dominates. The 78–19–3 split indicates the model expects Croatia to control play and secure three points more often than not, while draws remain plausible rather than unlikely.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices show marked divergence from the model. Both betting sources place Croatia's win probability near 51%, some 27 percentage points below the model's forecast. The market assigns Ghana roughly 19% win probability—nearly seven times higher than the model's estimate—and prices draws substantially higher at roughly 30%. This divergence is large enough to signal genuine disagreement between the statistical model and real-money markets. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is correct.
